calendar.matters:
Notice Of Petition To Compel Arbitration
Off Calendar Oct-28-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES - ATTY. DAVID BARRY , TEL. NO. 1-424-206-4303 APPEARING FOR THE PETITIONERS BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;
ATTY. HONG T. LE OF THE SQUIRE PATTON LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 1-415-743-2533 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; Petitioners Taner Pamuk and Sarah Hartmann's petition to compel arbitration is granted. The parties are required to arbitrate their disputes regarding the Jetta purchased by petitioners from respondent M & M Automotive Group, Inc. through either JAMS or Judicate West. The decision of which of these two organizations will be the arbitral forum may be made by respondent if it does so by the conclusion of the hearing on this motion. If respondent does not make the choice, then the arbitral forum will be JAMS, which appears to be petitioners' first choice. The arbitration provision in the parties' contract provides that the arbitral forum will be the American Arbitration Association "or any other organization to conduct the arbitration subject to our [respondent's] approval." The quoted language thus bestows a discretionary power on respondent affecting the rights of petitioners and under well-settled California law the implied covenant of good faith and fair deal imposes a duty on respondent to exercise its discretion in good faith and in accordance with fair dealing." (See, e.g., 300 DeHaro Street Investors v. Deaprtment of Housing Development (2008) 161 Cal. App, 4th 1240, 1253). Although petitioner has asked respondent to approve both JAMS and Judicate West, respondent has refused to approve either organization and has not offered any reason for its decision not to approve either one. The reasonable expectations of the parties based on the quoted contract language are that the parties desired that their disputes would be resolved in a fair and neutral arbitral forum. By rejecting JAMS and Judicate West without giving any reason for doing so, respondent has breached the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing in the parties' contract. By granting petitioners' motion, the court is providing a specific performance remedy for respondent's breach. Arbitration will be through JAMS . Discovery will be limited to two depositions. And anything other than that, the parties have the right to ask the arbitrator for additional discovery based on a showing of good cause. Petitioner to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976
